steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence ends “...oil ______ which was created in 1910 by American artist Lyonel Feininger.” The blank must name the painting (Uprising) and correctly punctuate the nonrestrictive “which” clause that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "painting Uprising,": places the comma directly after the title and before “which,” as required for a nonrestrictive relative clause. Correct. ✓
  • "painting Uprising": omits the comma before “which,” violating the rule that a nonrestrictive “which” clause must be preceded by a comma. ✗
  • "painting, Uprising": inserts a comma between “painting” and “Uprising,” breaking up the noun phrase incorrectly and still leaving no comma before “which.” ✗
  • "painting. Uprising.": the period after “Uprising” creates a fragment (“Uprising. which was created...”) since “which” cannot begin an independent sentence here. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
